title: How the System Works
subtitle: Technical reference for indicators, scoring methodology, and platform features.

scoring.title: Scoring System
scoring.p1: Every imported decision is evaluated with a score from 0 to 1, where 0 is the optimal decision and 1 is the maximum deviation from ideal. The score combines four weighted factors: hand equity, bet sizing accuracy, positional awareness, and MTT context (M-ratio and ICM pressure).

table.standard_meaning: Solid line. Minimal deviation from optimal.
table.marginal_meaning: Defensible action, but a slightly better alternative exists.
table.small_meaning: Minor strategic loss, relevant in the long run.
table.clear_meaning: Clear mistake with significant EV impact.

scoring.p2: The system considers MTT context when reclassifying decisions: under high ICM pressure (M ≤ 6 or final table), error margins are tighter — a marginal decision may be reclassified as small_mistake if the context amplifies the strategic cost.
scoring.p3: The Standard% of a tournament is the percentage of decisions labeled standard out of the total analyzed. It is the primary indicator of technical quality in a session.
scoring.example_standard: Correct fold of a weak hand OOP with insufficient pot odds — minimal deviation from optimal
scoring.example_marginal: Flop call with borderline equity — defensible action, but a slightly better alternative existed
scoring.example_small: Value bet with incorrect sizing for the board — minor strategic loss that compounds long-term
scoring.example_clear: River call with 20% equity against an 80% pot-sized bet — clear and costly mistake

indicators.title: Indicators
indicators.std_measures: % of decisions classified as standard in the period
indicators.std_interpret: Target: above 70%. Elite players stay above 92%.
indicators.avg_measures: Average raw score 0–1 across all decisions
indicators.avg_interpret: Lower is better. < 0.15 is excellent; > 0.30 indicates a high error volume.
indicators.clear_interpret: Directly linked to EV lost per session. Target: < 5%.
indicators.leakroi_measures: Estimated EV lost per leak spot in the period (in BBs)
indicators.leakroi_interpret: Prioritize leaks with the highest negative ROI — those cost the most.
indicators.icm_measures: ICM pressure level at decision time (low / medium / high)
indicators.icm_interpret: Incorrect decisions under high ICM cost more in the long run.
indicators.drift_measures: Quality decline trend across consecutive sessions
indicators.drift_interpret: Indicates tilt or fatigue. Reviewing recent sessions is recommended.
indicators.p1: Leak ROI is calculated by multiplying the frequency of each error spot by the average EV cost of an incorrect decision in that spot. High-frequency spots with low individual cost can outweigh rare spots with high cost — ROI captures that.

mstacks.title: M-Ratio Phases
mstacks.p1: Harrington's M measures how many full blind orbits (SB + BB + antes) your stack can sustain without playing a hand. It governs MTT strategy: the lower the M, the narrower the range of viable actions.
mstacks.deep_strategy: Full post-flop poker. Room for bluffs, slowplays, and stack maneuvers.
mstacks.mid_strategy: Pre-flop playability starts narrowing. Careful with big-stack confrontations.
mstacks.short_strategy: Focus on reshove/push-fold spots. Resteals important to maintain M.
mstacks.pushfold_strategy: Binary strategy: fold or all-in. ICM pressure automatically high.
mstacks.p2: Push/Fold decisions are evaluated with different criteria than other phases. The system applies push/fold tables based on minimum equity and position to calculate the correct score in this context.
mstacks.p3: Tournament stage (early, middle, late, final_table, heads_up) is detected by the number of active players at the table and complements M in defining the context.

dna.title: Decision DNA
dna.p1: Decision DNA is the player's strategic fingerprint — a 5-axis radar calculated from the complete decision history. It reveals structural game patterns independent of financial results.
dna.aggression_calc: % of aggressive actions (bet/raise) out of total post-flop
dna.fold_calc: % of decisions that resulted in a fold
dna.threebet_calc: % of pre-flop situations where a 3-bet occurred vs opportunities
dna.threebet_ref: Varies by position; > 12% in EP may be exploitable
dna.positional_calc: Aggression difference between late position and early position
dna.discipline_calc: % of decisions classified as standard (= Standard%)
dna.p2: The combination of axes generates an archetype: Nit, Passive Calling Station, LAG, TAG, Disciplined TAGfish, Aggressive Reg, or Balanced Reg. The archetype is a diagnostic starting point — not a definitive classification.

ghost.title: Ghost Table / Drills
ghost.p1: The Ghost Table converts your own leaks into review drills. Each "spot" is a recurring pattern where you made below-optimal decisions — the system extracts these spots automatically from your imported history.
ghost.p2: How the adaptive SRS works: after each drill session, the interval until the next review is adjusted based on performance:
ghost.mastery_next: Spot archived — maximum interval of 60 days
ghost.p3: The GhostDrillCard on the dashboard shows spots with overdue review (red), about to expire (yellow), and on track (green). The goal is to keep all spots green — a sign that the patterns have been internalized.
ghost.p4: XP earned: 25 XP per completed drill, 100 XP for reaching mastery on a spot.
ghost.p5: Pressure Mode adds a 30-second countdown per decision. An SVG ring displays the countdown; when it reaches zero, a fold is automatically recorded and the hit streak resets. The accumulated streak is shown with a 🔥 badge during the session and on the final summary tile.

compare.title: Tournament Comparison
compare.p1: Select 2 to 4 tournaments on the Tournaments page and click "Compare" to see a side-by-side analysis. The comparison shows:
compare.bullet_standard: Standard% and Avg Score for each tournament
compare.bullet_leaks: Top 3 active leaks per session with frequency and EV loss
compare.bullet_narrative: AI-generated narrative highlighting technical evolution or regression
compare.p2: The Delta (▲/▼) shows the change in an indicator between the reference tournament (oldest selected) and the others. Green means improvement, red indicates regression in the same spot.

coaching.title: Coaching
coaching.p1: When a coach is linked to your account, the system automatically creates a baseline — a snapshot of your indicators at the time of linking. All tournaments imported after that are compared against this baseline to measure real progress.
coaching.baseline_meaning: Average Standard%, Avg Score, and top leaks at the coaching start date
coaching.delta_meaning: Indicator changes since baseline — the primary progress metric
coaching.reviewed_meaning: The coach annotated or commented on at least one decision in the session
coaching.override_meaning: The coach replaced the AI-generated study plan with a custom one
coaching.effectiveness_meaning: Median Standard% improvement for students in the first 60 days of coaching
coaching.p2: Coach effectiveness is calculated by comparing each student's average Standard% in the 30 days before linking with the 30 days after the baseline. Coaches with ≥ 3 students with a baseline receive a verified public badge in the directory.

gamification.title: Gamification
gamification.p1: The XP system rewards study activity, not financial results. XP is persisted on the server and synced across devices.
gamification.event_drill: Complete a drill in the Ghost Table
gamification.levels_title: Level is calculated from the average Standard% of recent tournaments (minimum 5):
gamification.streak_desc: The number of consecutive days with registered activity (any XP-generating event). Resets if a day passes without activity.
gamification.ach_first: First Analysis — import and analyze the first tournament
gamification.ach_drill: First Drill — complete the first drill in the Ghost Table
gamification.ach_streak: Week of Focus — 7 consecutive days of streak

career.title: Career Trajectory
career.p1: The Career Trajectory card projects when you will reach each level based on your actual Standard% curve across imported tournaments. The system uses linear regression over recent tournaments to calculate improvement rate and estimate dates.

table.slope_meaning: Standard% improvement per tournament (e.g. +0.12% = grows 1.2% every 10 tournaments)
table.milestone_meaning: Standard% threshold to reach each level (e.g. Solid requires 86%)
table.blocking_meaning: High-frequency leak spots with the greatest impact on your current Standard%

career.p2: The dashed line in the chart represents the future projection — not a guarantee. If the slope is negative or zero, the system indicates stagnation and does not project dates.
career.p3: The AI narrative in the card summarizes: (1) current trend, (2) estimated time to the next level, (3) the priority leak to fix to accelerate progression.

cognitive.title: Cognitive Failure Mapper
cognitive.p1: The Cognitive Failure Mapper detects cognitive-emotional patterns in the chronological sequence of decisions within each tournament. Instead of looking at isolated decisions, the system analyses sliding windows of 5–10 consecutive decisions to identify when the player's mental state shifts.

patterns.revenge_signal: 2+ aggressive mistakes in the next 5 decisions
patterns.fear_signal: 2+ fold mistakes in the next 5 decisions
patterns.sunk_signal: Wrong call on 2+ streets of the same hand
patterns.compensation_trigger: Correct fold of a strong hand
patterns.compensation_signal: 2+ wrong calls in the next 3 decisions

cognitive.p2: Each pattern receives a frequency (% of opportunities where the pattern manifests) and a severity level — high (≥ 40%), medium (≥ 20%) or low (below 20%). Minimum: 30 decisions to activate.
cognitive.p3: The AI narrative describes the dominant pattern, why it costs EV, and one concrete corrective habit to apply in the next session.

twin.title: Strategic Patterns
twin.p1: The Strategic Twin turns spot-level error frequency data into predictive language. Instead of listing past mistakes, the system says: "in shove preflop spots under high ICM, you make errors 63% of the time — well above your personal average of 28%".
twin.p2: Each spot is defined by the combination of street (preflop/flop/turn/river), best_action (the correct action: fold/call/raise/bet/check/shove) and ICM pressure (low/medium/high/critical). The system filters spots with a minimum of 5 decisions and error_rate at least 10% above your personal average.

table.avg_meaning: Global percentage of decisions classified as small_mistake or clear_mistake in the last 180 days
table.costly_meaning: Spot where error_rate > personal average + 10% and volume ≥ 5 decisions
table.delta_meaning: How much the spot's error_rate exceeds (positive) or falls below (negative) your personal average

twin.p3: The vertical line in the bar chart represents your personal average. Bars that exceed this line indicate spots where you perform below your usual standard. The AI narrative summarises the 3 most costly tendencies and proposes one concrete adjustment.

leaks.title: Top Leaks Detected
leaks.p1: The Top Leaks card lists the most frequent and costly error patterns found in your history. A leak is a spot (combination of street, position and action) where you consistently make sub-optimal decisions — not an isolated mistake, but a habit.
leaks.p2: Each leak shows the frequency (how many times the pattern was detected), the average error score, and the estimated EV lost per month if the behaviour continues. Leaks marked as Critical have an average score above 0.36 — these cost the most real ROI.
leaks.p3: The trend indicator (Improving / Stagnant / Regressing) shows whether your behaviour in that spot has improved over your recent tournaments. A Regressing leak needs immediate attention — it means you have reverted to the same mistake after a better period.
leaks.p4: Use the Study button to open the spot's drill directly in the Ghost Table. Completing the drill and repeating until mastery is the most effective way to internalise the correction.
leaks.example: Real detected leak: 'River overcall OOP' — 31 occurrences, average score 0.44 (Critical), trend Regressing. The estimated EV lost represents the monthly cost if the pattern continues uncorrected.

causal_map.title: Leak Causal Map
causal_map.p1: The Causal Map visualises which leaks co-occur in the same sessions or hands. Each node is a leak; each line connecting two nodes indicates they appear together more often than expected. The line thickness represents the strength of the correlation.
causal_map.p2: Why it matters: when two leaks co-occur with high correlation, they usually share a root cause — for example, excessive preflop folding and bad flop calls may both stem from the same range-reading problem. Fixing the root cause resolves both at once.
causal_map.p3: Click a node to highlight only the leaks connected to it. Red nodes are critical (average score ≥ 0.36); yellow are moderate; green are minor. Always start with the red nodes that have the most connections — they have the greatest cascading impact.

form.title: Recent Form
form.p1: The Recent Form card shows average decision quality in recent tournaments, tournament by tournament. Each bar represents a session: green means a standard session, yellow is marginal, orange indicates small mistakes, and red signals a session with clear mistakes.
form.p2: Recent form matters because it captures short-term variation that the overall average hides. A player with a good overall average may be declining over the last 5 tournaments — an early warning sign of tilt, fatigue, or a stake change.
form.p3: If you see a sequence of red or orange bars, check the Cognitive Patterns card — there may be an active emotional pattern explaining the decline.
form.example: Last 7 tournaments: two solid sessions, followed by decline in sessions 3–5 (possible tilt or fatigue), then recovery in sessions 6–7 — a typical over-commitment cycle.

decisions.title: Decision Quality
decisions.p1: The Decision Quality card shows the percentage breakdown of all your decisions across the four quality levels: Standard, Marginal, Small Mistake, and Clear Mistake.
decisions.p2: This aggregate view reveals the overall profile of your game. A player with 80%+ Standard and under 5% Clear Mistakes has a solid profile. A high concentration of Clear Mistakes indicates spots where you are losing EV significantly and consistently.
decisions.p3: The connection to leaks: every Clear Mistake tracked here will appear in the Top Leaks card if the pattern is recurring. Reduce your Clear Mistake percentage by focusing drills on the most frequent spots.
decisions.example: Typical profile of a Regular player: 74% Standard, 16% Marginal, 7% Small Mistake, 3% Clear Mistake. This 74% Standard% positions the player between Grinder and Regular on the system scale.

streets.title: Performance by Street
streets.p1: The Performance by Street chart shows the standard decision rate (score ≤ 0.08) broken down by preflop, flop, turn, and river.
streets.p2: Streets with orange or red bars indicate where your leaks are concentrated. Most players lose the most on the river — where the pot is largest and mistakes are most expensive. Players who level up quickly typically fix preflop first (highest decision volume) and then the turn.
streets.p3: Use this card to prioritise which streets to focus on in the Ghost Table and Academy.
streets.example: Common pattern in intermediate players: Preflop 84% Standard, Flop 76%, Turn 70%, River 63%. The weaker river indicates difficulty with showdown decisions — fold equity vs. thin value bets.

positions.title: Performance by Position
positions.p1: The Performance by Position chart shows the standard decision rate by table position: UTG, MP, CO, BTN, SB, and BB.
positions.p2: Late positions (BTN, CO) naturally have more positional advantage — better performance is expected from those seats. If BTN or CO appear red, that is a serious issue: you are not taking advantage of the positional edge you have.
positions.p3: BB and SB defensive spots are technically the hardest. Poor performance in the BB is common and not necessarily a crisis — but if the delta between BTN and BB is very large, the positional deficit is costing real EV.
positions.example: BTN (84%) and CO (79%) make good use of positional advantage. BB (63%) and SB (60%) weaker — a 24pp delta between BTN and SB is elevated but expected. Above 30pp indicates a critical positional leak.

pressure.title: Pressure Collapse
pressure.p1: The Pressure Collapse card compares your decision quality across three ICM pressure contexts: no pressure (early tournament), medium pressure (late / near-bubble), and high pressure (bubble and final table).
pressure.p2: A technically solid player maintains the same score regardless of pressure. A delta above 8 points between high pressure and no pressure is a sign of collapse — you make significantly more mistakes when real money is at stake.
pressure.p3: Pressure collapse is one of the most expensive leaks in MTTs because it is precisely in high-pressure situations (bubble, FT) that decisions have the greatest monetary impact. If the card shows collapse, prioritise drills with high ICM context in the Academy.

table.none_meaning: Early tournament — small blinds, no immediate elimination pressure
table.low_meaning: Tournament in progress, outside bubble — decisions carry normal weight
table.medium_meaning: Near the bubble or with significant pay jumps
table.high_meaning: On the bubble, final table, or critical reshove situation

pressure.example: Left: only a 4pp delta between no-ICM and high-ICM — consistent performance, a sign of solidity. Right: an 18pp delta — mistakes spike significantly on the bubble and final table, where each chip has greater monetary value.

icm.title: ICM Pressure
icm.p1: The ICM Pressure card shows the distribution of your decisions by ICM pressure level: no ICM, low, medium, and high. Each slice represents the percentage of decisions made under that context.
icm.p2: ICM (Independent Chip Model) is a mathematical model that calculates the real monetary value of chips in an MTT. 1000 chips on the bubble are not worth the same as 1000 chips at the start of the tournament — ICM captures that difference.
icm.p3: Players who play many short-format tournaments (Sit & Go, Spin & Gold) will have a high proportion of high ICM — which amplifies the importance of getting pressure spots right. Compare this card with the Pressure Collapse card to understand whether you are gaining or losing EV in the most valuable situations.
icm.example: Typical Spin & Go distribution: 8% no ICM, 15% low ICM, 38% medium ICM, 39% high ICM. Most decisions occur under pressure — each reshove mistake carries proportionally greater cost than in long MTTs.

bankroll.title: Bankroll Evolution
bankroll.p1: The Bankroll Evolution chart shows the cumulative financial result curve tournament by tournament. The line rises on profitable results and falls on losses — the overall shape reveals your long-term trend.
bankroll.p2: Financial results are heavily influenced by variance in MTTs — especially with samples below 100 tournaments. A downward curve does not necessarily mean you are playing poorly; it may be variance. That is why the system uses Standard% as the primary quality indicator — it measures decisions, not results.
bankroll.p3: Use the period filters (1M, 3M, 1Y, All) to isolate trends in specific time windows. If Standard% is rising but the financial curve is falling, you are playing better — results tend to correct themselves with volume.
bankroll.p4: If both Standard% and the curve are falling simultaneously, review your most recent leaks and check whether you have recently changed stakes — buy-in changes alter field competitiveness.

level.title: My Level
level.p1: The My Level card shows your current level in the system, the progress percentage to the next level, and the leaks blocking your advancement.
level.p2: Level is calculated based on the average Standard% of your recent tournaments (minimum 5 to calculate). Each level requires a higher decision quality threshold — it is based on technical consistency, not financial results.
level.p3: The blocking leaks shown in the card are the highest-frequency error spots that most impact your current Standard%. Fixing them is the most direct path to levelling up. Use the View study plan button to see the AI-generated personalised plan.
level.example_progress: of the way to the next level
level.example: Grinder level at 73% Standard, 4pp below the Regular threshold (77%). Progress at 75% of the way. The blocking leak identified by the AI is the highest-frequency error spot preventing advancement.

sparring.title: Sparring Mode
sparring.p1: Sparring Mode (/sparring) turns passive learning into learning by action. Instead of reviewing an isolated spot, you play a complete historical hand, pausing at each real decision point — and the engine evaluates every choice with the same rigour as the Ghost Table.
sparring.phase_playing_desc: Each hand decision is presented with cards, board, and full context (position, stack, M-ratio, ICM, pot). The user chooses one of 6 available actions.
sparring.phase_feedback_desc: Immediate result: correct or incorrect, recommended action, score delta, SRS scheduling, and optional engine analysis via AI.
sparring.phase_summary_desc: After all decisions: overall accuracy, per-decision outcome summary, and a button to play a new hand.
sparring.p2: The auto-selected hand is the one containing the player's worst mistake in the last 90 days, preferring hands with multiple decisions to create a full narrative arc (preflop → flop → turn/river). Each new session excludes already-played hands from selection — the system cycles through distinct hands in the 90-day window; once all are exhausted, it restarts from the beginning.
sparring.p3: Every answer is registered in the SRS (Spaced Repetition) system: correct answers advance the review interval [3, 7, 14, 28, 60 days]; wrong answers reset to 3 days. Sparring integrates directly with the Ghost Table — the same spots appear in both modes as the SRS schedules them.
